name: Auth System
description: Generic authentication utilities with session handling abstraction, extensible permission system, and no role assumptions.

intent.what: Provides a complete authentication layer on top of Supabase Auth, including sign-up/sign-in, OAuth, MFA, admin operations, and middleware.
intent.why: Abstracts Supabase Auth into a clean, reusable API with proper error handling, session management, and extensible middleware.

code_examples: const { user } = await signInWithPassword({ email, password })

internal_flow: Get Supabase client from core layer
Handle errors and transform to AuthError

execution_reasoning.enforceAuth: Checks session, then applies role/verification rules
execution_reasoning.admin: Uses service role client when available

anti_patterns: Don't store passwords in plain text
Don't skip email verification for sensitive apps
Don't use admin operations without service role key

when_to_use: Use enforceAuth() to protect API routes
Use MFA functions for enhanced security
Use admin functions for user management
when_not_to_use: Don't bypass enforceAuth() for protected routes
reasoning_strategy: Determine auth flow needed (password, OAuth, magic link)
Handle AuthError with appropriate HTTP status
